scope: Actual-LI layer-2 compressed dKV many-to-one accumulation wall.

what_23p813x_means.read: The 23.813x number is only the modeled coalesced-write target for S4096 layer2 with token_block=32, selected_block=512, and d_block=16. It divides raw query-major dKV atomic elements by tile-coalesced compressed-group/D write elements.

new_dense_group_tile_probe.mechanism: One CTA per batch/seq_tile/selected_tile/D16 tile keeps a dense compressed-group x D16 table in LDS, accumulates selected token contributions locally, then emits sparse global atomics for touched groups.
new_dense_group_tile_probe.contract: Consumes diagnostic materialized probs and grad_logits; validates the compressed dKV accumulation/write path only. It is not the final fused sparse-MLA/LI backward kernel.
new_dense_group_tile_probe.why_tested: It checks whether local accumulation can realize the write-reduction ladder before investing in CK-Tile/MFMA segmented accumulation.

s512_layer2.read: Local accumulation is a real improvement over scalar global-style HIP at this size, but remains slower than materialized GEMM-shaped PyTorch reference.

s4096_layer2_sb256_timing_only.read: This is the lower-pressure selected_block=256 bring-up rung. It halves the selected span, so it gets about half the full 23.813x write-reduction target. Runtime is far too slow because the body is scalar and contends in LDS/local atomics. rocprof confirms the HIP kernel itself is the wall, not Python setup or memory copies.

decision.keep: The tile-local accumulation contract and the 32x512/D16 primary target remain valid.
decision.reject: Do not pursue this scalar dense-LDS table as a production kernel. It proves correctness and some S512 directionality, but S4096 timing is not viable.
decision.next: Implement the same accumulation contract with MFMA-shaped query-major recompute and segmented/local dKV reduction, preferably CK-Tile or equivalent. The kernel must avoid dense route materialization, preserve query-major selected-key reuse, and reduce global dKV writes inside the tile before touching global memory.
decision.target_gate: A viable next candidate must beat the existing materialized BMM floor directionally while removing its 4-8 GiB route/prob workspace; otherwise it will not close the sparse-attention backward gap toward the Ascend-style target.
